phrases.intent_en: Ask about tours of the stadium
phrases.text_local: Est-ce qu'il y a des visites guidées du stade ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess-KEEL ee ah day vee-ZEET gee-DAY doo STAHD
phrases.cultural_note: The Olympic Stadium, built for the 1976 Summer Olympics, offers guided tours of its iconic inclined tower and retractable roof.

responses.english: There are tours every day at one o'clock.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah day vee-ZEET too lay ZHOOR ah TREZ UR
responses.french: Il y a des visites tous les jours à treize heures.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about taking the tower observatory
phrases.text_local: Je voudrais monter à l'observatoire de la tour olympique.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DREH mon-TAY ah lob-sair-vah-TWAHR duh lah TOOR oh-lam-PEEK
phrases.cultural_note: The Olympic Tower's funicular takes visitors to an observation deck 175m above the ground for panoramic views of Montréal.

responses.english: The observatory is open, take the elevator over there.
responses.phonetic: lob-zair-vah-TWAHR eh too-VAIR pruh-NAY lah-sahn-SUR lah-BAH
responses.french: L'observatoire est ouvert, prenez l'ascenseur là-bas.

phrases.text_local: Où est le stationnement le plus proche ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo EH luh stah-syon-MAH luh ploo PROSH
phrases.cultural_note: 'Stationnement' is the Québécois word for parking (vs. 'parking' used in France). The Olympic Park has large parking areas.

responses.phonetic: luh stah-syohn-MAHN eh soor luh bool-VAR PEE-NURF
responses.french: Le stationnement est sur le boulevard Pie-IX.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what events are on this week
phrases.text_local: Quels événements ont lieu cette semaine au stade ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel ay-vayn-MAH on LYUH set suh-MEN oh STAHD
phrases.cultural_note: The stadium hosts concerts, trade shows, flea markets, and occasional sporting events. The giant Marché des Antiquités is very popular.

responses.english: It is posted at the entrance.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how to get there by metro
phrases.text_local: C'est quelle station de métro la plus proche ?
phrases.pronunciation: say kel stah-SYON duh may-TROH lah ploo PROSH
phrases.cultural_note: The Pie-IX and Viau metro stations on the Green Line both serve the Olympic Park. The metro is called 'le métro' just like in France.

responses.english: Take the green line, Pie-IX station.
responses.phonetic: pruh-NAY lah LEE-nyuh VART stah-SYOHN PEE-NURF
responses.french: Prenez la ligne verte, station Pie-IX.
